target.title: fix(agents): strip plaintext model provider keys
target.body:
## Summary
- strip plaintext provider `apiKey` values after merge-mode preservation, source-managed enforcement, and provider compatibility transforms so old `models.json` keys cannot be reintroduced into the final prompt-facing catalog
- clean legacy catalog-only `models.json` plaintext provider keys before the empty-provider `skip` path can preserve them
- migrate existing `models.json`-only plaintext provider keys into the target agent's local `auth-profiles.json` before writing the stripped catalog when there is no configured provider key and no target-local auth profile for that provider
- keep inherited main-agent profiles and runtime/external overlays from suppressing local legacy `models.json` key migration for non-main agents
- preserve legacy env-name catalog credentials such as `LITELLM_KEY` by migrating them to env-backed auth-profile `keyRef`s instead of inline literal keys
- preserve non-secret markers such as env SecretRef markers and custom-provider `secretref-managed` compatibility markers
- add regression coverage for generated plaintext stripping, merge-mode stripping, catalog-only cleanup, existing-key migration, fail-closed behavior, inherited-profile migration boundaries, and pi model discovery compatibility

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Generated and legacy `models.json` files should not persist plaintext provider `apiKey` values. Cleanup should preserve upgrade auth outside the prompt-facing catalog, including a non-main agent whose local legacy catalog credential would otherwise be masked by an inherited main-agent auth profile.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source from this PR branch at `d0690950`, macOS, Node.js `v24.1.0`, `/usr/bin/python3` for fs-safe pinned writes. Provider keys in proof output are redacted.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
FS_SAFE_PYTHON_MODE=require FS_SAFE_PYTHON=/usr/bin/python3 node --import tsx --input-type=module
```

The inline Node proof script created temporary local agent dirs, called `ensureOpenClawModelsJson`, `discoverModels`, `ensureAuthProfileStore`, and `createProviderAuthResolver`, and exercised these real paths: inline custom provider stripping, legacy configured `models.json` key migration, catalog-only cleanup, env-name `keyRef` migration, non-main inherited-profile migration boundary, and fail-closed persistence.

- Evidence after fix (copied live output, redacted):

```json
{
  "head": "d0690950",
  "tempRoot": "<tmp>",
  "inlineCustomProvider": {
    "apiKeyMarker": "secretref-managed",
    "hasPlaintextInModelsJson": false,
    "discoverModelsFindsCustomModel": "custom-model"
  },
  "legacyModelsJsonOnlyUpgrade": {
    "apiKeyMarker": "secretref-managed",
    "hasPlaintextInModelsJson": false,
    "migratedProfile": {
      "type": "api_key",
      "provider": "custom",
      "copyToAgents": false,
      "displayName": "Migrated from models.json",
      "key": "<redacted>"
    },
    "discoverModelsFindsLegacyModel": "legacy-model"
  },
  "catalogOnlyLegacyCleanup": {
    "apiKeyMarker": "secretref-managed",
    "hasPlaintextInModelsJson": false,
    "migratedProfile": {
      "type": "api_key",
      "provider": "custom",
      "copyToAgents": false,
      "displayName": "Migrated from models.json",
      "key": "<redacted>"
    },
    "discoverModelsFindsCatalogOnlyModel": "catalog-only-model"
  },
  "envNameCatalogMigration": {
    "modelsJsonPromptSafe": true,
    "migratedProfile": {
      "type": "api_key",
      "provider": "custom",
      "hasInlineKey": false,
      "keyRef": {
        "source": "env",
        "provider": "default",
        "id": "LITELLM_KEY"
      },
      "copyToAgents": false
    },
    "runtimeAuth": {
      "mode": "api_key",
      "source": "profile",
      "profileId": "custom:models-json",
      "apiKeyMarker": "LITELLM_KEY",
      "resolvedEnvValueSeen": true
    }
  },
  "inheritedProfileBoundary": {
    "modelsJsonPromptSafe": true,
    "hasWorkerPlaintextInModelsJson": false,
    "localMigratedProfile": {
      "type": "api_key",
      "provider": "custom",
      "copyToAgents": false,
      "displayName": "Migrated from models.json",
      "key": "<redacted>"
    },
    "runtimeAuth": {
      "mode": "api_key",
      "source": "profile",
      "profileId": "custom:models-json",
      "resolvedWorkerKey": true
    }
  },
  "failClosedMigrationPersistence": {
    "threw": true,
    "message": "Failed to migrate existing models.json provider apiKey for custom; keeping existing models.json unchanged",
    "modelsJsonUnchanged": true
  }
}
```

- Observed result after fix: the final `models.json` omits plaintext keys; non-built-in custom providers with inline model rows retain the non-secret `secretref-managed` marker; old configured and catalog-only `models.json` credentials migrate into `auth-profiles.json`; env-name legacy catalog values migrate as env-backed `keyRef` credentials and resolve through provider auth; a non-main agent with an inherited same-profile ID still migrates its local legacy catalog key and runtime auth resolves the worker-local key; pi `discoverModels` can still find custom model rows; and when auth-profile persistence cannot complete, the existing `models.json` remains unchanged instead of being stripped without a durable auth profile.
- What was not tested: I did not test a real paid third-party provider. The proof covers product `models.json` generation, legacy configured cleanup, legacy catalog-only cleanup, auth-profile migration, inherited-profile migration boundary, fail-closed persistence behavior, and pi model discovery compatibility with temporary local agent dirs and redacted dummy keys.

## Tests
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/models-config.write-serialization.test.ts --maxWorkers=1 --reporter=dot`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/models-config.plan.test.ts --maxWorkers=1 --reporter=dot`
- `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false -p tsconfig.core.json`
- `NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=8192 pnpm exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/models-config.ts src/agents/models-config.write-serialization.test.ts src/agents/models-config.plan.ts src/agents/models-config.plan.test.ts src/agents/auth-profiles.ts src/agents/auth-profiles/store.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/agents/models-config.ts src/agents/models-config.write-serialization.test.ts src/agents/models-config.plan.ts src/agents/models-config.plan.test.ts src/agents/auth-profiles.ts src/agents/auth-profiles/store.ts`
- `git diff --check`

Refs #11829
target.author: pcdqc
